prior_visit_summaries: 55yo male with acute epigastric pain radiating to back, nausea and vomiting. Vitals BP 110/65, HR 90, RR 18, Temp 36.8. Physical exam: epigastric tenderness. Labs: lipase 260 U/L, triglycerides 350 mg/dL, BUN 32 mg/dL, creatinine 1.8 mg/dL, glucose 280 mg/dL. Ultrasound: post‑cholecystectomy, sludge in ducts, dilated common bile duct 8 mm. Admitted for acute pancreatitis; started IV normal saline 3L/24h, insulin infusion 0.1 U/kg/hr, ondansetron 4 mg IV q8h, NPO status, pending CT abdomen and possible ERCP.

Follow‑up gastroenterology visit on 2026‑03‑25: mild epigastric pain and improved nausea; CT abdomen with contrast shows slightly swollen pancreas, mild peripancreatic fat stranding, mild common bile duct dilation. Labs trending downward: lipase 180 U/L, triglycerides 275 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, creatinine 1.7 mg/dL, glucose 260 mg/dL. Continuing IV fluids, insulin infusion, prednisone 15 mg daily, azathioprine 150 mg daily, low‑carbohydrate diet and clear liquids as tolerated, ondansetron PRN. Plan for repeat laboratory tests (lipase, triglycerides, CMP, CRP, ESR) and possible imaging if CBD remains dilated. Outpatient gastroenterology follow‑up in 7 days.

2026‑03‑26 ICU admission for acute deterioration with severe epigastric pain, fever, hypotension. Labs: lipase 1,260 U/L, triglycerides 1,020 mg/dL, WBC 18,000/µL, CRP 130 mg/L. Initiated IV lactated Ringer's 150 mL/h, insulin infusion 0.1 U/kg/hr, piperacillin‑tazobactam 4.5 g IV q6h. Prednisone and azathioprine held. Continuous monitoring with daily labs (CBC, CMP, lipase, triglycerides, CRP, cultures). Plan for ERCP once hemodynamically stable, with nephrology consult for CKD monitoring.

Repeat contrast CT showed marked pancreatic enlargement with hypodense necrotic areas in the head and body, a 5x4 cm peripancreatic fluid collection near the tail, and a dilated CBD 8 mm with suspected sludge. Patient remained in ICU with severe epigastric pain and hemodynamic instability; management included continued insulin infusion, lactated Ringer's, broad‑scope antibiotics, planned urgent ERCP when stable, daily labs, renal monitoring, NPO status, and suspension of prednisone and azathioprine.

2026‑03‑28: Post‑ERCP ICU assessment. Patient in septic shock with Enterococcus faecium bacteremia. Labs: creatinine 2.6, BUN 48, bilirubin 1.2. Initiated vancomycin 1 g IV q12h, insulin infusion 10 U/h, albumin 20 g IV bolus, continued lactated Ringer’s 150 mL/h. Hold prednisone/azathioprine; continue monitoring labs, consider dialysis if oliguria. Repeat imaging in 48–72 h. NPO maintained until GI function improves.

2026‑03‑29: Intubated for moderate ARDS secondary to septic shock from Enterococcus faecium bacteremia; intubated with lung‑protective ventilation and prone positioning. Started vasopressor support to maintain MAP ≥65 mmHg. Insulin infusion titrated to 12 U/h. Added daptomycin 6 mg/kg IV q24h. Administered albumin 25 g IV daily. Ongoing vancomycin and broad‑scope antibiotics. Daily labs and cultures obtained; renal function monitored; continuous renal replacement therapy considered if oliguria <400 mL/day or refractory acidosis. Early enteral nutrition to be initiated when hemodynamics permit. Alcohol cessation counseling and addiction support arranged. Follow‑up with ICU monitoring, nephrology, infectious disease, GI, and psychiatry.

2026‑03‑30: Interventional radiology percutaneous drainage of peripancreatic necrotic collection; ongoing vasopressor support with norepinephrine; continued CRRT and insulin infusion; broad‑scope antibiotics vancomycin, daptomycin; levofloxacin added; culture of drain output grew Enterococcus faecium; plan to monitor drainage output, adjust antibiotics, maintain support, repeat CT in 48–72 h.

2026‑03‑31: Nephrology visit – initiated continuous venovenous hemofiltration for anuric acute kidney injury secondary to septic shock. Continued broad‑scope antibiotics including vancomycin, daptomycin, meropenem, levofloxacin, piperacillin‑tazobactam. Insulin infusion maintained at 12 U/h; albumin 20 g IV bolus given. Vasoactive support with norepinephrine to maintain MAP ≥65 mmHg. Daily labs (serum creatinine, BUN, electrolytes, CBC, lactate) and urine output monitoring. Plan to wean CRRT as renal function improves and follow‑up with outpatient nephrology.

2026‑04‑01: Radiology reassessment of persistent necrotizing pancreatitis with septic shock. Patient remained NPO, on broad‑scope antibiotics (meropenem, vancomycin, daptomycin), insulin infusion, albumin, norepinephrine, and CRRT. Labs showed marked transaminitis (AST 540, ALT 620). Repeat contrast CT on 2026‑03‑30 showed persistent pancreatic necrosis, no new fluid collections. Plan continued antimicrobial therapy, daily LFT monitoring, repeat CT in 48–72h, possible percutaneous drainage if imaging worsens, continued CRRT, weaning of vasopressors when stable, early enteral nutrition, opioid analgesia, alcohol cessation counseling.

2026‑04‑02 Palliative Care visit: Family meeting reviewed goals of care. Patient remained in ICU on mechanical ventilation, continuous venovenous hemofiltration, norepinephrine, insulin infusion, and broad‑spectrum antibiotics (vancomycin, daptomycin, meropenem, levofloxacin, piperacillin‑tazobactam) with percutaneous drainage of necrotic pancreatic collection. Immunosuppressants prednisone and azathioprine permanently withheld. Daily ICU rounds planned with ongoing monitoring of organ function; preparation for comfort‑only care if further deterioration, including initiation of opioids and anti‑emetics.

2026‑04‑03: Patient died after transition to comfort‑only care; mechanical ventilation, continuous venovenous hemofiltration, vasopressors, antibiotics, and other life‑sustaining therapies were withdrawn.
